candidates.topicTitle: GitHub Copilot CLI combines model families for a second opinion
candidates.summary: GitHub introduced a Copilot CLI capability that pulls in a different model family for a second opinion. The feature is notable because even the tooling is now acknowledging that one answer is often not enough for real engineering work.
candidates.whyTrendingNow: Fresh GitHub Blog launch on 2026-04-06 with immediate developer interest around reliability and model disagreement.
candidates.audienceResonance: Engineers using coding assistants, platform teams evaluating AI tooling, and technical leaders trying to separate useful workflow gains from demo theater.

candidates.summary: Docker announced general availability for Docker Offload, aimed at developers working in constrained environments where Docker Desktop is difficult or impossible to run locally. It is a practical response to the reality of VDI, managed desktops, and enterprise device limits.
candidates.whyTrendingNow: Fresh Docker launch on 2026-04-02 with clear relevance to enterprise development workflows and remote execution.
candidates.audienceResonance: Software engineers, platform teams, consultants, and CTOs dealing with onboarding drag, local environment friction, and corporate desktop constraints.

candidates.topicTitle: A macOS bug that causes TCP networking to stop working after 49.7 days
candidates.summary: A detailed write-up describes a macOS TCP networking bug that detonates after a long uptime window. It is exactly the kind of absurd systems problem that reminds developers why operational humility still matters.
candidates.whyTrendingNow: The post gained strong Hacker News attention because the bug is bizarre, specific, and painfully relatable to anyone who has debugged networking failures.
candidates.audienceResonance: Backend engineers, SREs, systems programmers, and experienced developers who know the most annoying bugs are often the least glamorous ones.

candidates.topicTitle: Claude Code is unusable for complex engineering tasks with the Feb updates
candidates.summary: A highly visible GitHub issue argues that recent Claude Code updates made the tool worse for complex engineering work. The discussion captures a broader frustration with AI tools that regress under real-world load.
candidates.whyTrendingNow: The issue drew major Hacker News and GitHub attention because it speaks to reliability problems engineers hit after the honeymoon phase of AI tooling.
candidates.audienceResonance: Teams actively using coding agents, engineering leads setting expectations, and skeptical developers tired of workflow regressions.

candidates.topicTitle: What’s coming to our GitHub Actions 2026 security roadmap
candidates.summary: GitHub laid out a security roadmap for Actions focused on secure defaults, policy controls, and CI/CD observability. The post matters because software supply chain protection is increasingly becoming a platform-level expectation, not an optional extra.
candidates.whyTrendingNow: Recent roadmap post with strong relevance after continued supply chain incidents and rising scrutiny on CI/CD trust boundaries.
candidates.audienceResonance: Platform engineers, security teams, engineering managers, and founders who need to harden build pipelines without killing developer velocity.
